message:
fix(daemon): use rpc.ShortSocketPath() consistently for socket path construction

Status/health/metrics/doctor functions were using filepath.Join(beadsDir, "bd.sock")
while the actual daemon uses rpc.ShortSocketPath() which produces a hash-based path
in /tmp to avoid the 104-char Unix socket limit on macOS. This caused these functions
to look for the socket at the wrong path and fail to connect on long workspace paths.

Fixed in: prime.go, create.go, activity.go, daemon_lifecycle.go, doctor/daemon.go,
doctor/fix/daemon.go. Also fixed a variable shadowing bug in showDaemonHealth() where
beadsDir was re-derived from the socket path (wrong when socket is in /tmp).

Closes: bd-5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/polecats/topaz
Rig: beads
Role: polecats

files.patch:
@@ -596,7 +596,7 @@ func discoverRigDaemons() []rigDaemon {
 		beadsDir = resolveBeadsRedirect(beadsDir)
 
 		// Check if daemon is running
-		socketPath := filepath.Join(beadsDir, "bd.sock")
+		socketPath := rpc.ShortSocketPath(filepath.Dir(beadsDir))
 		client, _ := rpc.TryConnect(socketPath)
 
 		rigName := routing.ExtractProjectFromPath(route.Path)

@@ -794,7 +794,7 @@ func flushRoutedRepo(targetStore storage.Storage, repoPath string) {
 
 	// Construct paths for daemon socket and JSONL
 	beadsDir := filepath.Join(targetBeadsDir, ".beads")
-	socketPath := filepath.Join(beadsDir, "bd.sock")
+	socketPath := rpc.ShortSocketPath(targetBeadsDir)
 	jsonlPath := filepath.Join(beadsDir, "issues.jsonl")
 
 	debug.Logf("attempting to flush routed repo at %s", targetBeadsDir)
